text: The video successfully builds and maintains high excitement through rapid action sequences, but suffers from pacing issues during static title cards and extended pre-order screens. To improve retention, condense the early title card and integrate the pre-order information more dynamically to prevent viewer drop-off before the final post-credits scene.
reasoning: Patience hits its lowest point (0.3) at 10 seconds (Chunk 3) due to an extended, static title card. Conversely, excitement peaks at 55 seconds (1.0) during rapid transitions between red-lit corridors and desert environments. The pre-order screens at 120-130 seconds (Chunks 25 and 26) cause a massive spike in boredom (0.8) and a drop in excitement (0.2). The post-credits action scene at 130 seconds is highly effective but risks being unseen if viewers drop off during the static promo screens.
keyFindings: Early static title cards (Chunk 3) significantly drop patience and increase boredom.
Rapid environmental transitions and high-octane combat (Chunks 11-16) drive peak excitement.
Static pre-order screens (Chunks 25-26) cause the highest boredom levels in the entire video.
Post-promo action (Chunks 27-28) successfully re-engages viewers but risks low viewership due to preceding boredom.
suggestions: Shorten the 'Call of Duty: Black Ops 7' title card in Chunk 3 to under 2 seconds.
Overlay the 'RISING TENSIONS' text in Chunk 1 directly onto action footage rather than using a standalone card.
Animate the pre-order incentive screens in Chunks 25-26 to maintain visual momentum.
Condense the pre-order promo duration from 10 seconds to 5 seconds.
Move a snippet of the post-promo action (Chunk 27) into the pre-order screen as a background video to retain viewers.
